title: hibbertia horricomis
intro: Hibbertia horricomis, also known as Rough Guinea Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae, native to Australia. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Hibbertia horricomis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Hibbertia horricomis can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
